communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New York Freedom of Information Law, I hereby request the following records:

All contract documents between the NYPD and Ntrepid.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophie Hayssen

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I, Sophie Aren Hayssen, request copies of all property leases by the federal government in New York state that are active as of March 27, 2026.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sophie Hayssen

title: June 9, 1996 Prosecution File and Booking Photos (Fu C. Wang)

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: June 9, 1996 Prosecution File and Booking Photos (Fu C. Wang)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

All records held by the Suffolk County District Attorney’s Office relating to the prosecution of:

Fu C. Wang
Date of Arrest: June 9, 1996
Arresting Agency: Boston Police Department
Case No.: 9601CR003699 Commonwealth vs. Wang, Fu C
Charges: 266/30/A-0 - Felony LARCENY OVER $250 c266 §30(1) ; 266/37B/K-0 - Misdemeanor - 100 days or less incarceration CREDIT CARD, RECEIVE STOLEN c266 §37B(b)

I have attached the Boston Police Department's arrest/incident report and a copy of the Massachusetts Trial Court Record for the case for your reference. 

Please note the Boston Police Department Media Relations Office advised me that the Suffolk County District Attorney’s Office may be the appropriate custodian for the requested booking photograph and related prosecution records.

Accordingly, I request the following:

Any booking photograph or mugshot contained within the prosecution file.
Any photographic exhibits introduced at arraignment, grand jury, or trial.
The docket sheet and charging documents (complaint, indictment, or information).
Any publicly releasable portions of the prosecution case file that include arrest documentation or identification materials.
Any index, archive reference number, or retention log identifying the location or status of the file if stored off-site.

If the District Attorney’s Office does not maintain the original booking photograph but retains documentation indicating the originating agency or archive location, I request that such information be provided.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Peter Barth
